skill.name: azure-ai-textanalytics-py
skill.description: Azure AI Text Analytics SDK guidance for Python. Covers sentiment analysis, named entities, key phrases, language detection, PII detection, healthcare NLP, batch actions, and async clients.
skill.summary: Use Azure AI Text Analytics from Python for common NLP workflows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported a critical heuristic and many command, script, credential, and cryptography patterns. Review found these are primarily Markdown code examples, SDK imports, placeholder Azure configuration, and false matches on text such as key phrases. The remaining low risk is that users may send text, PII, or healthcare content to Azure and examples read credentials from environment variables.

low_findings.title: Cloud NLP Examples May Process Sensitive Text
low_findings.description: The skill documents Azure Text Analytics calls for PII detection and healthcare NLP. This is expected for the SDK, but users may send regulated or confidential text to Azure if they run the examples without governance controls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples explicitly cover PII and healthcare text analytics. The risk is privacy and compliance exposure, not malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Credential Access Is Documented
low_findings.description: The examples read AZURE_LANGUAGE_ENDPOINT and AZURE_LANGUAGE_KEY from environment variables. This is normal Azure SDK usage and does not expose secrets by itself, but users should avoid printing or committing these values.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The credential reads are visible and expected for Azure authentication. No evidence shows exfiltration or logging of the credential values.

low_findings.title: Azure Endpoint Placeholder Indicates Outbound Service Use
low_findings.description: The documented endpoint is a placeholder for an Azure Cognitive Services resource. This is legitimate for an Azure SDK skill, but it means analyzed text is sent to a configured cloud service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint format is clearly an Azure service URL placeholder. It confirms network use but not suspicious exfiltration.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: Most external command findings are Markdown code fences or inline method names. The only real shell instruction is pip install for the Azure package, and it requires explicit user execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation snippets and table entries, not code executed by the skill. No automatic shell invocation was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dynamic Import Finding Is a Static False Positive
dangerous_patterns.description: The script finding points to a Python import list for Azure Text Analytics action classes. It is not a dynamic import expression and does not execute untrusted code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The line contains a normal Python from-import statement in a Markdown example. There is no importlib, eval, exec, or user-controlled module name.

dangerous_patterns.title: Sensitive File And Weak Crypto Findings Are Text Matches
dangerous_patterns.description: The high-severity findings map to ordinary documentation text such as key phrases, package metadata, and method tables. No certificate files, private keys, hashing code, or cryptographic operations are present.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are SDK descriptions and NLP method names. I found no evidence of certificate material, key files, MD5, SHA1, or similar cryptographic implementation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Critical Heuristic Not Confirmed By Context
dangerous_patterns.description: The dangerous combination heuristic combines documented package installation, Azure endpoint configuration, and environment credential examples. Review found no prompt injection, obfuscation, unauthorized network destination, or credential exfiltration intent.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The individual signals are legitimate Azure SDK setup steps. No hidden instructions, data exfiltration, or executable payload beyond examples was found.

content.value_statement: Python developers need reliable examples for Azure AI Language features. This skill provides focused SDK patterns for sentiment, entities, PII, healthcare NLP, batch jobs, and async use.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to install the azure-ai-textanalytics Python package.
Demonstrates API key and Entra ID authentication patterns.
Provides examples for sentiment analysis with opinion mining.
Covers entity recognition, key phrase extraction, and language detection.
Shows PII detection, redaction, and healthcare entity analysis.
Includes batch action and async client usage patterns.
limitations: Requires an Azure AI Language resource and valid credentials.
Does not provision Azure resources or manage access policies.
Examples are documentation snippets and are not a complete application.
Users must handle compliance requirements for PII and healthcare text.

use_cases.title: Add Sentiment Analysis To An App
use_cases.description: Use the Python SDK examples to classify customer feedback and inspect opinion mining results.

use_cases.title: Extract Entities From Documents
use_cases.description: Apply entity recognition and key phrase extraction to summarize business text and support search workflows.

use_cases.title: Review PII And Healthcare Text
use_cases.description: Use redaction and healthcare entity examples while planning privacy controls and approval workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Start With Sentiment
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a first Text Analytics call.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use azure-ai-textanalytics-py to show me the minimal Python steps for sentiment analysis with Azure AI Language.

prompt_templates.title: Add Authentication
prompt_templates.scenario: Choose between API key and Entra ID authentication.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use azure-ai-textanalytics-py to compare API key and Entra ID authentication for TextAnalyticsClient in Python.

prompt_templates.title: Design A Batch Pipeline
prompt_templates.scenario: Plan multiple analyses on the same document set.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use azure-ai-textanalytics-py to outline a batch pipeline that runs entities, key phrases, and sentiment on product feedback.

prompt_templates.title: Harden Sensitive Text Handling
prompt_templates.prompt: Use azure-ai-textanalytics-py to design a safe PII and healthcare text workflow with Azure Text Analytics and Python.

output_examples.input: Analyze two customer reviews for sentiment.
output_examples.output: The assistant explains the client setup, shows the sentiment call, and describes positive, negative, and neutral confidence scores.

output_examples.input: Find entities and key phrases in a product announcement.
output_examples.output: The assistant describes how to run entity recognition and key phrase extraction, then explains how to process returned labels and phrases.

output_examples.input: Plan PII redaction for support tickets.
output_examples.output: The assistant outlines PII detection, redacted text review, secret handling, and privacy checks before production use.

best_practices: Use Entra ID where possible and keep API keys out of source control.
Batch documents when appropriate, and handle per-document errors explicitly.
Review privacy and retention requirements before sending sensitive text to Azure.
anti_patterns: Do not paste real secrets into prompts, code examples, or logs.
Do not send PII or healthcare text without an approved data handling policy.
Do not assume language detection or entity extraction is always correct.

faq.question: What does this skill help me build?
faq.answer: It helps you use Azure AI Text Analytics from Python for common natural language processing tasks.

faq.question: Does it require Azure credentials?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples require an Azure AI Language endpoint and either an API key or Entra ID credential.

faq.question: Can it detect personally identifiable information?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents PII detection and redaction patterns provided by Azure AI Text Analytics.

faq.question: Does it include healthcare text analytics?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes an example for healthcare entity analysis using the Azure Text Analytics client.

faq.question: Does the skill run code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides Markdown examples that users can adapt and run in their own environment.

faq.question: What should I check before production use?
faq.answer: Confirm authentication, error handling, batching limits, privacy controls, and Azure service configuration.
